ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Horlicks Ice; closest parent is malted milk beverage (FOODON_03301638).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKK covers malted beverages as a category; no specific Horlicks Ice code exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for iced Horlicks malt beverage.

preparation_method: mixed with hot water or milk

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Horlicks Ice is approximately 250 ml (one cup or glass), as typically served at Malaysian and Singaporean kopitiams and hawker stalls.

nutrient_source.title: Horlicks Original Malted Drink — Nutritional Information (per 100 g powder); adapted for prepared beverage with whole milk at standard dilution
nutrient_source.organization: GlaxoSmithKline / Haleon — Horlicks product label data; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for malted milk beverage (NDB 14209)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Horlicks Ice is prepared by dissolving Horlicks malt powder in hot water or milk and serving over ice. Nutrient values per 100 g of prepared beverage are estimated from USDA FoodData Central NDB 14209 (malted milk beverage, prepared with whole milk), which is the closest approved analogue. Horlicks product label macros were used to cross-validate powder composition. Values reflect the prepared drink at approximately 3 heaped teaspoons (22 g powder) per 200 ml whole milk, yielding ~222 ml; scaled to per-100 g basis.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; malted milk beverage analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Horlicks Ice as a prepared iced beverage. GI of 45 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for malted milk beverages and Horlicks-type drinks, which report GI values in the range 37–55 (low category). A midpoint of 45 is used. Glycemic load per 250 g serving = (45 × 10.2 × 2.5) / 100 = 11.5 g. This is consistent with the low-to-moderate GL range for milk-based malt drinks.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~130 mg per 250 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: A single serving of Horlicks Ice contributes approximately 130 mg sodium, which is within acceptable limits for hypertensive individuals when consumed occasionally, but regular daily consumption alongside other sodium-containing foods should be monitored.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugars and moderate glycemic load (~11.5 per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Horlicks Ice contains approximately 21 g of sugar per 250 ml serving and has a glycemic load of approximately 11.5; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size, avoid adding condensed milk, and account for this beverage within their daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat and moderate sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Horlicks Ice prepared with low-fat milk provides a relatively low saturated fat profile (~4.3 g per serving) and is not a significant cardiovascular risk when consumed in moderation as part of a balanced diet, though versions made with full-fat or condensed milk increase saturated fat and caloric load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Horlicks Ice is a popular cold malt-based beverage widely consumed in Malaysia and Singapore, particularly at kopitiams (traditional coffee shops) and hawker centres. It is prepared by dissolving Horlicks malt powder — a wheat-based malted barley and milk powder blend fortified with vitamins and minerals — in hot water or warm milk, then pouring the mixture over ice to create a chilled drink. The beverage has a distinctive sweet, malty, and slightly creamy flavour with a smooth texture. It is commonly made with whole milk or sweetened condensed milk, which increases its caloric density and sweetness. Horlicks Ice is a staple comfort drink across all age groups in the region and is often ordered alongside kaya toast or nasi lemak. Nutritionally, a 250 ml serving provides approximately 198 kcal, 9 g protein, 7 g fat, and 25.5 g carbohydrate, along with meaningful amounts of calcium (~325 mg), vitamin B12, and vitamin D from the fortified powder and milk base. Its glycemic index is estimated at 45 (low category), making it moderately suitable for blood sugar management when consumed without added condensed milk. Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious of the sugar content, particularly when condensed milk is added. The drink is vegetarian and halal-certified in most commercial preparations.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Horlicks Ice?
Is Horlicks Ice suitable for diabetics?
What is the GI of Horlicks?

confidence_reason: Horlicks Ice as a prepared iced beverage is not directly listed in any approved primary nutrient database. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central NDB 14209 (malted milk beverage prepared with whole milk), which is the closest approved analogue. GI is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for malted milk drinks. Confidence is medium rather than low because the Horlicks powder composition is well-documented on product labels and the preparation method is standardised.
notes: Nutrient values are per 100 g of prepared Horlicks Ice beverage (Horlicks powder dissolved in whole milk, served over ice). trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA analogue source for this beverage category; naturally zero in the milk-and-malt base at standard preparation. dietary_fibre_g is 0.1 — trace amount from malt powder; confirmed trace per USDA FoodData Central NDB 14209. monounsaturated_fat_g (-1 sentinel not used; value estimated from USDA analogue). All micronutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central NDB 14209 and Horlicks product label fortification data. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for malted milk beverages; no direct measurement for Horlicks Ice exists in the approved GI database. Serving size of 250 g is based on standard kopitiam cup volume. Versions prepared with sweetened condensed milk will have significantly higher sugar and caloric content than reported here. The cuisine_tags in the input (Noodle, Dosa) appear to be erroneous metadata from the source platform and have not been carried forward.
